title: Surface errors to users: replace console.error with toast notifications + loading states
analysis:
The task requires replacing 8+ console.error calls with user-visible toast notifications, adding a loading/error state during app initialization, and integrating Tauri's native save dialog for export. Currently:

1. **App.tsx** has 6 console.error sites: getInitialState failure (line 127), subscribeToEvents failure (line 140), file-change subscription failure (line 158), setParameter failure (line 170), re-evaluate failure (line 102), reload failure (line 207). No loading state during init.
2. **Editor.tsx** has 4 console.error sites: save failure (line 82), updateSource failure (line 99), LSP didChange failure (line 109), LSP init failure (line 133). The Editor operates internally and doesn't have a toast mechanism.
3. **ExportDialog.tsx** already handles errors with toasts via handleDoExport in App.tsx but uses a hardcoded path `export.${format}` instead of a native file picker.

**Approach:**
- Create a `useToast` hook (createToast factory following SolidJS conventions) that provides `showToast(message, type)` with auto-dismiss. This centralizes toast logic currently spread across App.tsx's manual signals.
- Replace App.tsx console.error calls with toast notifications using `showToast`.
- Add `initPhase` signal to App.tsx: 'loading' | 'ready' | 'error'. Show a loading spinner/skeleton during init, error state with retry on failure.
- For Editor.tsx errors (save, LSP init): pass an `onError` callback prop from App.tsx so the Editor can surface errors as toasts without coupling to toast implementation. This follows the existing dependency-injection pattern (onSelect, onSetParameter, etc.).
- For export: add a `pickSavePath` bridge function that invokes a backend command. Since the Tauri dialog plugin is NOT installed (no @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog in package.json or Cargo.toml), we add the bridge function with graceful fallback to hardcoded path when the backend command doesn't exist yet.
- LSP didChange/fileSwitch errors stay as console.error since they're frequent recoverable internal operations that would flood the user with noise if toasted.

Existing infrastructure to reuse: Toast component (gui/src/panels/Toast.tsx) is already fully built with data-type styling. App.module.css already has .toastContainer with fixed positioning. The Show component pattern from SolidJS is used throughout for conditional rendering.

prerequisites.description: Create useToast hook file skeleton at gui/src/hooks/useToast.ts (empty export to enable test-first development)

steps.description: Write failing tests for useToast hook in gui/src/__tests__/useToast.test.ts: (1) showToast sets message and type signals, (2) dismissToast clears message to null, (3) auto-dismiss fires after 5000ms for success type, (4) auto-dismiss fires after 10000ms for error type, (5) calling showToast again resets the auto-dismiss timer, (6) dismissToast cancels pending auto-dismiss timer. Use createRoot from solid-js for reactive context, vi.useFakeTimers for timer assertions.

Implement createToast() in gui/src/hooks/useToast.ts: createSignal<string|null>(null) for message, createSignal<'success'|'error'|'info'>('info') for type. showToast(msg, type) sets both signals and starts setTimeout (5000ms for success/info, 10000ms for error). dismissToast() clears message and cancels timer. Returns { toastMessage, toastType, showToast, dismissToast }.

Write failing tests for App.tsx loading state in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx (new describe 'App initialization loading state'): (1) shows data-testid='app-loading' while getInitialState is pending (use deferred promise), (2) shows data-testid='app-error' with retry button when getInitialState rejects, (3) clicking retry button calls getInitialState again, (4) after successful getInitialState resolve, data-testid='app-layout' is shown and loading/error elements are gone.

Add loading/error state to App.tsx: createSignal<'loading'|'ready'|'error'>('loading') for initPhase. Wrap onMount init logic in try/catch that sets 'ready' on success, 'error' on failure. Add conditional rendering with Show: when loading show spinner div (data-testid='app-loading'), when error show error div with Retry button (data-testid='app-error'), when ready show normal layout. Add retry handler. Add .loading and .errorState CSS classes to App.module.css.

Write failing tests for App.tsx toast on setParameter failure in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx: modify/replace existing 'handleSetParameter error handling' test. When bridgeSetParameter rejects, a toast element (data-testid='toast' with data-type='error') appears in the DOM containing the error message. Verify console.error is NOT called (behavior replaced).

Wire createToast() hook into App.tsx, replacing manual toastMessage/toastType/setToastMessage/setToastType signals. Replace console.error in handleSetParameter (line 170) with showToast(`Parameter update failed: ${err.message}`, 'error'). Update Toast rendering in JSX to use toast.toastMessage()/toast.toastType()/toast.dismissToast().

Write failing test for App.tsx toast on re-evaluate failure in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx (new describe 'App re-evaluate error toast'): mock bridgeUpdateSource to reject, simulate keyboard shortcut trigger (or directly call the handler via store interaction), verify error toast appears.
Replace console.error in onReEvaluate callback (App.tsx line 102) with showToast('Re-evaluation failed: ...', 'error').

Write failing test for App.tsx toast on reload failure in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx (new describe 'App reload error toast'): set changedFile, mock bridgeOpenFile to reject, trigger handleReload via ReloadPrompt interaction, verify error toast appears.
Replace console.error in handleReload (App.tsx line 207) with showToast('Reload failed: ...', 'error').

Write failing test for App.tsx toast on event subscription failure in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx (new describe 'App event subscription error toast'): mock engineStore.subscribeToEvents to reject, verify warning toast appears after mount ('Event subscription failed — some updates may not appear').

Replace console.error for subscribeToEvents failure (App.tsx line 140) and onFileChanged failure (App.tsx line 158) with showToast warning calls. These are non-fatal: show info/error toast but continue running.

Write failing tests for Editor.tsx error surfacing via onError prop in gui/src/__tests__/Editor.test.tsx: (1) new describe 'Editor save error callback' — when saveFile rejects, onError callback is called with message string, (2) new describe 'Editor LSP init error callback' — when lspClient.initialize rejects, onError is called with LSP failure message.

Add optional onError?: (message: string) => void to EditorProps in Editor.tsx. Replace console.error in save handler (line 82) with props.onError?.('Failed to save file: ...') call. Replace console.error in LSP init chain (line 133) with props.onError?.('LSP initialization failed — completions and diagnostics may be unavailable'). Wire onError from App.tsx to Editor component, passing (msg) => showToast(msg, 'error').

Write failing tests for export with save dialog in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx or gui/src/__tests__/ExportDialog.test.tsx: (1) handleDoExport calls bridge.pickSavePath before bridgeExportGeometry, (2) if pickSavePath returns null (user cancelled), bridgeExportGeometry is NOT called, (3) if pickSavePath throws (not available), falls back to default path and proceeds with export.

Add pickSavePath to bridge.ts: invoke<string|null>('pick_save_path', { format, defaultName }). Update handleDoExport in App.tsx to call pickSavePath first wrapped in try/catch; if result is null, abort (user cancelled); if call throws, fall back to hardcoded 'export.{format}' path. This way the feature degrades gracefully when the backend command doesn't exist yet.

Write integration test in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx verifying end-to-end flow: App renders, getInitialState succeeds (ready state), then setParameter fails, verify toast is visible with correct error message. Ensures the createToast hook is properly wired through the full component tree.

Final cleanup pass: verify all console.error calls in App.tsx are replaced with toast calls, ensure Editor.tsx LSP didChange/fileSwitch errors remain as console.error (intentional), run full vitest suite to confirm no regressions. Clean up any unused imports.

Write failing tests for initApp race condition guard in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx (new describe 'App initApp concurrent execution guard'): (1) 'rapid double-click on Retry does not start two concurrent initApp flights': first call to getInitialState returns a deferred promise, click Retry, then immediately click Retry again while first is still pending — verify getInitialState is called only twice total (initial mount + first retry), not three times. (2) 'retry cleans up prior subscriptions before re-subscribing': mock onMeshUpdate etc. with tracked unlisten fns, let first initApp succeed (subscriptions established), then make getInitialState reject on second call, click Retry — verify the prior unsub/fileChangedUnsub are called (cleaned up) before the second initApp attempts to subscribe. (3) 'Retry button is disabled while initApp is in-flight (loading phase)': after getInitialState rejects and error state shows, click Retry — verify the Retry button becomes disabled (or disappears) synchronously while loading, preventing a second click from firing.

Fix the initApp race condition in App.tsx with a two-pronged approach: (1) At the top of initApp(), clean up existing subscriptions before proceeding: call `unsub?.(); unsub = undefined; fileChangedUnsub?.(); fileChangedUnsub = undefined;` — this ensures that if initApp is called again (via retry), any previously-established subscriptions are torn down so they cannot leak or fire duplicates. (2) The Retry button is already gated by initPhase: since initApp() synchronously sets initPhase to 'loading' at line 121, the Show block for the error state (which contains the Retry button) is removed from the DOM immediately on click, preventing a second click from reaching handleRetry. Verify this is sufficient by confirming the Show condition `initPhase() === 'error'` is mutually exclusive with 'loading'. If the Retry button is still clickable during microtask gap, additionally add `disabled={initPhase() === 'loading'}` to the Retry button as a belt-and-suspenders guard. Run tests from step-19 to confirm all pass.

design_decisions.decision: Create a reusable createToast() hook rather than adding toast logic inline in App.tsx
design_decisions.rationale: App.tsx already has manual toastMessage/toastType signals (lines 61-62). Extracting to a hook adds auto-dismiss, centralizes logic, and makes it testable in isolation. The factory-function pattern (createToast) follows the existing SolidJS convention used by createEditorStore, createEngineStore, createSelectionStore.

design_decisions.decision: Surface Editor.tsx errors via an onError callback prop rather than importing toast hook directly
design_decisions.rationale: The Editor component is a leaf component that shouldn't know about the toast system. Dependency injection via callback prop is consistent with the codebase pattern (onSelect, onSetParameter, onGroupDoubleClick, etc.) and keeps the Editor testable without mocking toast infrastructure.

design_decisions.decision: Keep LSP didChange/fileSwitch errors as console.error (not toasted)
design_decisions.rationale: These are frequent, recoverable internal operations that fire on every keystroke (debounced). Toasting every LSP sync error would flood the user with noise. Only LSP init failure (which means broken completions/diagnostics for the session) warrants a user-visible warning.

design_decisions.decision: Add pickSavePath to bridge.ts with graceful fallback to hardcoded path
design_decisions.rationale: The Rust backend doesn't have a pick_save_path command yet — the Tauri dialog plugin is not in Cargo.toml dependencies. By adding the bridge call with try/catch fallback, we prepare the frontend for the native dialog while maintaining current behavior until the backend implements the command.

design_decisions.decision: App init loading state uses a simple 'loading' | 'ready' | 'error' signal with conditional rendering via Show
design_decisions.rationale: SolidJS's Show component + signal is the idiomatic pattern. No need for a separate store — init state is local to App.tsx lifecycle. The three-state enum is sufficient: loading (pending), ready (success), error (failed with retry).

design_decisions.decision: Auto-dismiss timers: 5s for success/info, 10s for errors
design_decisions.rationale: Success messages are brief acknowledgements; errors need more reading time and may contain diagnostic information. The timer resets when showToast is called again to prevent stale messages from lingering.

design_decisions.decision: Fix initApp race condition via subscription cleanup + Show-based button removal rather than an explicit inFlight flag
design_decisions.rationale: The initPhase signal already drives conditional rendering via Show — when initApp sets initPhase('loading') synchronously at entry, SolidJS removes the error state (and its Retry button) from the DOM, preventing a second click. This is the idiomatic SolidJS approach and avoids adding a separate boolean flag. The critical fix is cleaning up existing unsub/fileChangedUnsub at the top of initApp so that even if concurrent execution somehow occurs (e.g., programmatic call), prior subscriptions are always torn down before new ones are established. This prevents subscription leaks and duplicate event handlers.

reuse.where: Already renders toast UI with message, type, and dismiss button
reuse.how: Keep as-is. The new createToast hook manages state; Toast component remains a pure presentational component. No changes needed to Toast.tsx itself.

reuse.where: Already has fixed positioning, z-index, and width constraints for toast overlay
reuse.how: Reuse directly. Only add .loading and .errorState CSS classes for the new init states.

reuse.what: Existing App.test.tsx mock patterns for bridge functions
reuse.where: vi.mock('../bridge') with all bridge functions mocked, waitFor pattern for async assertions
reuse.how: Follow the same mock setup pattern for new tests. Add pickSavePath to the mock list.

reuse.where: All bridge functions follow invoke<T>('command', params) pattern
reuse.how: Add pickSavePath following the same pattern: invoke<string|null>('pick_save_path', { format, defaultName }).

reuse.what: Dependency injection callback pattern for component props
reuse.where: Used throughout: EditorProps, PropertyEditor (onSetParameter), ConstraintPanel (onConstraintSelect), Viewport (onSelect)
reuse.how: Add onError?: (message: string) => void to EditorProps following this established pattern.

reuse.what: SolidJS Show component for conditional rendering
reuse.where: Used in ExportDialog, App.tsx toast rendering, ReloadPrompt
reuse.how: Use Show with when={} for loading/error/ready state conditional rendering in App.tsx.

reuse.where: createEditorStore, createEngineStore, createSelectionStore all use factory functions returning {state, ...methods}
reuse.how: createToast follows the same convention: factory function returning reactive accessors + mutation methods.

reuse.what: Existing deferred promise test pattern for async race condition tests
reuse.where: Already used in App.test.tsx 'App async mount/cleanup race conditions' and 'App initialization loading state' describes
reuse.how: Follow the same pattern: create deferred promises for getInitialState/onMeshUpdate, control resolution timing to test concurrent execution scenarios.
